narrative: A ransomware operator compromises a privileged support path into the manufacturing control plane, disrupts plant scheduling, and forces recovery coordination across IT and operations.
enhancedNarrative: Suggested draft: A ransomware-driven outage affects the manufacturing control plane and adjacent scheduling services, leading to plant disruption, recovery effort, contractual pressure, and regulatory scrutiny.

metricSemantics.eventLoss: Conditional loss if a materially successful event occurs.
metricSemantics.annualLoss: Annualized loss after applying event frequency and event success logic across the year.

assumptions: Assumes plant scheduling remains the main point of impact across the selected geography.
Assumes current control strength and attacker capability are reasonably represented by the selected FAIR ranges.
Assumes linked risks can escalate together and justify the applied scenario uplift.

confidence.label: Moderate confidence
confidence.summary: This assessment is useful for management discussion, but some assumptions still need tightening before stronger reliance.
reasons: The scenario has been structured into asset, threat, attack type, and effect.
Internal citations or source material are linked to the assessment.
improvements: Tighten the event-frequency range using internal incident history or SME input.
Narrow the loss ranges with finance and operations input.

upward: Threat frequency is materially lifting annual exposure because the working case assumes about 1.38 events per year.
Business interruption is one of the biggest cost drivers in the current scenario.
stabilisers: Current control strength is helping contain the scenario and reduce success likelihood.

sensitivity.label: Event frequency
sensitivity.why: Frequency is a direct multiplier on annual loss in this linked scenario.

sensitivity.label: Event success likelihood
sensitivity.why: Threat capability versus control strength determines how often attempts convert into loss.

sensitivity.label: Business interruption
sensitivity.why: Business interruption is the largest per-event cost component in the current estimate.

assumptions.text: Assumes the manufacturing control plane remains the main point of impact across the selected geography.
Assumes ransomware-capable threat actors remain realistic during the assessment period.
Assumes the selected linked risks can escalate together and justify the applied scenario uplift.

scenarioTitle: Manufacturing control-plane ransomware disruption — Treatment case
narrative: The same ransomware scenario with stronger privileged-access controls, tested restore paths, and tighter segmentation around the manufacturing control plane.

assumptions: Assumes the treatment plan materially strengthens privileged-access and restore performance.
Assumes segmentation reduces interruption severity in the treatment case.

confidence.label: Moderate confidence
confidence.summary: This treatment case is useful for decision discussion because the improvement levers are explicit, but the recovery and interruption assumptions still need validation before committing investment.
reasons: The treatment case keeps the same baseline scenario, making the comparison easier to explain.
The improved control and resilience assumptions are directionally consistent with the stated treatment plan.
improvements: Validate the expected recovery-time improvement with operations and infrastructure owners.
Confirm whether the stronger privileged-access controls are funded and realistically achievable in the target period.

upward: Business interruption still remains the largest single cost driver, even after the treatment changes.
Annual exposure still depends on how quickly the service can be restored under pressure.
stabilisers: Stronger control strength materially reduces the chance of successful ransomware progression.
Lower secondary-loss assumptions help keep the severe tail lower than the baseline.

sensitivity.label: Business interruption
sensitivity.why: Service restoration assumptions still have the biggest effect on the severe-event result.

sensitivity.label: Control effectiveness
sensitivity.why: The improved control posture is one of the main reasons the treatment case drops below the warning trigger.

sensitivity.label: Event frequency
sensitivity.why: The treated case also benefits from fewer credible opportunities for a successful event to escalate.

uncertainty.label: Business interruption range
uncertainty.why: The treatment case still depends heavily on how much downtime can realistically be removed.

uncertainty.label: Recovery execution
uncertainty.why: The result assumes restore and containment are materially faster than in the baseline.

assumptions.text: Assumes stronger privileged-access controls are implemented and operated consistently.
Assumes tested restore paths materially reduce service interruption and recovery effort.
Assumes segmentation and recovery planning reduce the spread and duration of the outage.
